Title: Photometric Determination of Unresolved Main-sequence Binaries in 
       the Pleiades: Binary Fraction and Mass Ratio Distribution 
Authors: Liu .R., Shao Z., Li L.
Table: Results of Multiband Fitting for Stars with P_k_ {>=} 0.5
================================================================================
Byte-by-byte Description of file: ajada380t4_mrt.txt
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   Bytes Format Units   Label  Explanations
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   1- 18 I18    ---     Gaia   Gaia DR3 identifier
  20- 25 F6.4   solMass M1     ? Best-fit primary mass value
  27- 31 F5.3   ---     q      ? Best-fit mass ratio value; M2/M1
  33- 38 F6.4   solMass M1,16  ? The 16% quantile for the primary mass
  40- 45 F6.4   solMass M1,50  ? The 50% quantile for the primary mass
  47- 52 F6.4   solMass M1,84  ? The 84% quantile for the primary mass
  54- 58 F5.3   ---     q16    ? The 16% quantile for the mass ratio
  60- 64 F5.3   ---     q50    ? The 50% quantile for the mass ratio
  66- 70 F5.3   ---     q84    ? The 84% quantile for the mass ratio
  72- 76 F5.3   ---     Pb     ? Probability of a star being a binary (1)
  78- 91 A14    ---     Flag   Exclusion flag(s) (2)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Note (1): Derived from the probability density function.
Note (2): Indicates the criteria used for excluding field stars and 
          non-main-sequence members. 
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
